stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
You are cruising at FL340, and your aircraft's maximum certified altitude is FL382. Your planned route intersects a cold front, as depicted in the attached image. Given this situation, what is the most appropriate course of action?

options.a: Use your airborne weather radar and visual cues to circumnavigate the cells. Turbulence has to be expected.
options.b: Fly straight across the front, as apart from moderate/severe turbulence, NO other flight hazard is expected.
options.c: Climb to FL380 and fly above the cloud tops, where only light turbulence can be expected.
options.d: Ask ATC for help, because they have better weather radar available.
